To a stirred solution of N-Boc-piperidine (185 mg, 1.00 mmol, 1.0 equiv) in DCM (5 mL) was added trifluoroacetic acid (1.14 g, 10.0 mmol, 10 equiv) dropwise at 0 °C. The reaction was warmed to room temperature and stirred for 2 h. The mixture was concentrated under reduced pressure, then neutralized with saturated K2CO3 (10 mL) and extracted with DCM (3 × 10 mL). The combined organic layers were dried over Na2SO4, filtered, and concentrated to afford piperidine as a clear oil (78 mg, 92%).
